title: Crisp Tunisian Lamb Tagine
description: A tangy and crisp Tunisian dish featuring lamb, english mustard, and a blend of spices, perfect for a date night, this recipe is also gluten-free friendly and cooked to perfection in a traditional tagine pot.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the english mustard, olive oil, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Remove the lamb from the marinade and place it in the tagine pot.
Add the chopped onion to the tagine pot and cook over medium heat until the onion is softened.
Cover the tagine pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake the tagine for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the meat is falling apart.
Increase the oven temperature to 400 degrees Fahrenheit and continue baking for an additional 20 minutes, or until the lamb is crispy and browned.
Remove the tagine from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
